perturbation.original_text: (b) Renewal Term. If Affiliate fails to notify Network of its desire that this Agreement terminate on its expiration date, at least six (6) months before the expiration date, this Agreement will automatically renew, upon the same terms and conditions, for an additional four (4) -year period ("Renewal Term").
perturbation.changed_text: (b) Renewal Term. If Affiliate fails to notify Network of its desire that this Agreement terminate on its expiration date, at least one (1) month before the expiration date, this Agreement will automatically renew, upon the same terms and conditions, for an additional four (4) -year period ("Renewal Term").
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the notification period for termination to one month may be deemed unreasonably short, especially given the length of the initial term and the renewal period.  This creates a situation where the affiliate has very little time to consider its options and provide adequate notice, potentially conflicting with general principles of contract law requiring reasonable notice periods.
perturbation.law_citation: UCC § 2-309. ABSENCE OF SPECIFIC TIME PROVISIONS; NOTICE OF TERMINATION.
perturbation.law_explanation: UCC § 2-309(3) implies reasonable notification, which is contradicted by a one-month notice period for a multi-year agreement renewal. This modification introduces uncertainty regarding the enforceability of the renewal term, as courts may deem the notice period insufficient.

perturbation.justification: The change from a six-month to a one-month notification period for terminating an agreement that automatically renews for four years creates a contradiction under the principles of reasonable notification. The Law Explanation highlights UCC § 2-309(3), implying reasonable notification. The Perturbation Explanation also supports this by noting the unreasonably short notice period, especially given the length of the renewal term. While specific laws regarding notice periods are not found in the provided snippets, the general principle of 'reasonable notification' inferred from the Law Explanation and Perturbation Explanation indicates that a one-month notice is likely insufficient, potentially making the renewal term unenforceable. The modified text reduces the time to consider options, contrasting with established legal standards.

perturbation.original_text: (b) Network shall submit to Affiliate, within forty-five (45) days of the end of each calendar quarter during the Term, a statement reporting for such calendar quarter the following information on a Station-by-Station basis: (i) Network's Advertising Revenue, (ii) the Affiliate Advertising Share, (iii) Network's Transactional Revenue, (iv) the average number of households receiving the Service through each MVPD in each DMA served by a Station, as calculated herein, and (v) the Affiliate Transactional Share. If this Agreement terminates on any date other than at the end of a calendar quarter, Network shall supply such statement as of the date of termination, within forty-five (45) days thereafter, and this obligation shall survive the termination of this Agreement until Affiliate receives such statement.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) Network shall submit to Affiliate, within ninety (90) days of the end of each calendar quarter during the Term, a statement reporting for such calendar quarter the following information on a Station-by-Station basis: (i) Network's Advertising Revenue, (ii) the Affiliate Advertising Share, (iii) Network's Transactional Revenue, (iv) the average number of households receiving the Service through each MVPD in each DMA served by a Station, as calculated herein, and (v) the Affiliate Transactional Share. If this Agreement terminates on any date other than at the end of a calendar quarter, Network shall supply such statement as of the date of termination, within one-hundred and eighty (180) days thereafter, and this obligation shall survive the termination of this Agreement until Affiliate receives such statement.
perturbation.explanation: Extending the deadline for financial reporting significantly without reasonable justification could violate the principle of 'good faith and fair dealing' implied in contract law. While not explicitly illegal, unreasonably delaying the provision of crucial financial information impacts the Affiliate's ability to make informed business decisions and receive timely payments, potentially leading to financial harm.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Implied Duty of Good Faith and Fair Dealing
perturbation.law_explanation: This contract is governed by the laws of the State of New York (see section 13(c)), which has adopted UCC 1-304. Extending the financial reporting deadline to 90 days after the end of the calendar quarter and 180 days after termination without reasonable justification could violate the principle of 'good faith and fair dealing', which is implied in New York contract law.

Obligation of Good Faith. Every contract or duty within this act imposes an obligation of good faith in its performance and enforcement.
perturbation.justification: The modified text extends the financial reporting deadline, which, according to the Law Explanation, could violate the principle of 'good faith and fair dealing' implied in New York contract law, particularly UCC 1-304. Scraped Law Snippet 2, referencing UCC 1-304, states, "Every contract or duty within this act imposes an obligation of good faith in its performance and enforcement." By significantly extending the deadline without reasonable justification, the modified contract arguably undermines this obligation of good faith, thus creating a contradiction.
